reasoning: VIX at 20.33 (MID regime), falling -2.4% = primary bullish macro signal; fear receding into normal compression zone. Zero macro events today = clean environment, no forced no-trade windows. Overnight gap flat (-$0.24) indicates market held support after prior-day 1.87% SPY decline—no panic gap down, suggesting overnight absorbed selling. Per macro framework: VIX falling + no major events + gap not down = BULLISH bias. However, sector rotation remains risk-off (XLK -2.86%, XLF -2.29%, XLE -1.75%) and ribbon bear stack (spread 180¢) signal underlying caution. Macro backdrop is cautious relief, not true risk-on. Confidence 0.62 reflects bullish VIX direction but tempered by persistent risk-off rotation—favor bullish setups but with restraint.
